title: antholyza rupestris
intro: Antholyza rupestris, also known as Mountain Flax or Alpine Lupine, is a flowering plant species found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits also include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from rheumatism. Antholyza rupestris is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil for its medicinal purposes, and can also be found in some traditional herbal remedies and supplements.
